Video.summary: Stacey rolls Heavy Metal back into the shop and begins to lay out some of the incredible modifications that he is going to be doing on this old junk yard Æ69 International Cab over truck. A custom four-link air suspension goes on first, and then a slick air brake system to pull this big monster to a stop. A custom exhaust is also laid out, along with numerous other modifications to make this a one of a kind rig. If you have ever wondered if a Cab over could make a cool hot rod, you need to check this out. From there itÆs off to the movies as Stacey digs into the impact and influence movie cars have on our lives and culture. To prove that point, He brings in actor Dax Shepard and his cool Æ67 Lincoln Continental who are both starring in the new movie ōHit and Runö. So strap in and hold on, æcuz we might get airborne on this ride!
Video.title: Project Heavy Metal Suspension & Dax Shepard "Hit and Run" Lincoln Continental

Image.alt: Project Heavy Metal Suspension & Dax Shepard "Hit and Run" Lincoln Continental
Project Heavy Metal Suspension & Dax Shepard "Hit and Run" Lincoln Continental
Project Heavy Metal Suspension & Dax Shepard "Hit and Run" Lincoln Continental

Video.summary: Stacey and the crew hit the ground running with a late model replacement bed from LMC thatÆs sure to revive and old Ford farm truck and get it out of the pastures and back on the highway. Then itÆs off to show budding Jeep enthusiasts a couple of entry level options they can use when it comes to modifying their vehicle. We get a visit from a couple who learn how to install new seat upholstery. StaceyÆs found something cool for all you second generation Camaro owners. And weÆre just getting started! So get ready to turn some wrenches on GearZ.
Video.title: New Bed Old Ford

Video.summary: The GearZ crew and Cornwell Tools team up to help a new shop in Arkansas get some much-needed tools to help them take theyÆre shop the next level. Then Stacey puts together the final pieces of the Sgt. Rock project by making a trip to France and the beaches of Normandy. Then itÆs back to the shop to show you how easy it is to put fuel injection on your muscle car. ItÆs all hereģ and only on GEARZ!
Video.title: Gearz On The Road

Video.summary: Season 10 Starts off with AmericaÆs favorite military tribute truck! Sgt. Rock is back and ready for final assembly! Garage Guru, Stacey David, takes us on a tour de force of custom fabrication as he covers all the nuts and bolts of what itÆs taken to get Sgt. Rock ready to rumble. Builders of all levels are in for a great show as Stacey tackles one of a kind steering columns, gas pedals and designing a console that not only fits form, but also function. Gauge techs will keep your instrument panels in line. And a quick tip will keep your wires from fraying and your hoses from splitting. All this and more as Sgt. Rock and Season 10 hit the road full throttle.
Video.title: Nuts Bolts Building Sgt Rock

Video.summary: A custom build can lose some power if the theme doesnÆt stay consistent. Stacey shows how to do that by taking you through the process of how to make door and kick panels that keep the rugged military WWII bomber vibe flowing throughout the interior of Sgt. Rock. After that, its some tips on how to fit a radiator and that all-important cooling fan in a place where space is limited. Finally, the gas tank and battery placement are critical on a custom vehicle and Stacey goes through the steps in doing that as well. If youÆve always wanted to build a custom vehicle, this episode is sure to get your fabrication gearZ turning.
Video.title: Sgt Rock Interior Other Details

Video.summary: Two things are certain in life: death and taxes. We all know what to do with taxes, but what happens when a loved one passes and leaves behind an unfinished project vehicle? How do you know whatÆs sitting in your garage? And how can you find out? Stacey tackles these questions and takes you on a thorough inventory of an almost finished restoration that was left to a widow when her husband suddenly died. A new dash going into your vehicle? A gauge tech should keep you from crossing your wires. And weÆve got tips that should help with battery relocation and lining up your vehicleÆs body panels.
Video.title: Asessing Unfinished Project

Video.summary: The GearZ crew mount up and head out to visit a young gear-head whose love of wrenching started with 4-wheelers and has grown to include 4-wheel drive pick ups. Young Adam has a few surprises in store as Stacey visits him in his DadÆs garage and discusses tools, storage and tearing apart things to see how they work. Then, itÆs back in the shop as Stacey takes on the big question every gear-head has: What OTHER tools should I have in my box? A look beyond the typical wrenches, hammers and clamps will show you what you may need and what you may not, depending on your project.
Video.title: Gearz On The Road - Basic Tools

Image.alt: Gearz On The Road - Basic Tools
Gearz On The Road - Basic Tools
Gearz On The Road - Basic Tools

Video.summary: The son of legendary car builder, Bill Thomas, talks shop about wrenching, racing and his fatherÆs Cobra Killerģthe Cheetah. Then Stacey heads over to Stainless Works to check out how the CheetahÆs custom side pipes, headers and mufflers came together. A Gauge Tech explains the pros and cons of mechanical vs. electric gauges. After that, a Quick Tip explains the proper way to use some of those mystery tools laying around in your tool box.
Video.title: Cheetah Body Work Latches Custom Exhaust

Image.alt: Cheetah Body Work Latches Custom Exhaust
Cheetah Body Work Latches Custom Exhaust
Cheetah Body Work Latches Custom Exhaust

Video.summary: Ever wonder why your wife or best buddy wonÆt step foot in your late model truck? It could be that crusty interior. Tag along as Stacey takes on the sights and smells of an Æ83 Chevy in dire need of some attention. From new dash to leather on the bench seat and new carpet in the floor, Stacey shows you what a difference a weekend and some parts from LMC can make. Speaking of trucks, Stacey raises the bar with a bed lifter that will make you the most popular guy in your neighborhood. A new buffer is going to help add some shine to your vehicle and a creeper thatÆs simply ōMagicö is sure to raise an eyebrow or two. Big things are happening in the garage this week on GearZ.
Video.title: Old Dog New Truck

Video.summary: Through the years, weÆve had some amazing vehicles featured on GearZŚfrom elite racecars to the insanity of a V-8 barstool. Well, here we go again! Stacey hauls in a Thiokol Imp that needs someģattention. First things first with this crazy snowcatmobile; weÆve got to see if we can make it run. Follow along as Stacey takes you through the ins and outs of getting a neglected vehicle up and running again. For you performance BMW guys, a tuner from Dinan is going add the horses youÆve been looking for. WeÆve got sparks flying, fuel splashing and all kinds of garage mayhem this week on GearZ.
Video.title: Reviving An Old Snowcat

Video.summary: In the world of car building there are few things more ominous than missing titles or VIN numbers. But there is one task that stands out, one that brings grown men to tears, one that is usually lurking behind 100 yards of electrical tapeģWIRING. Well hold on tight as Stacey dives head first into wiring Sergeant Rock. ThatÆs right; see how painless Stacey can make wiring the big old truck. Follow along as Stacey moves front to back and gets the Rock one step closer to hitting the road!
Video.title: Wiring Sgt Rock

Video.summary: Everybody knows that 80Æs and 90Æs cars are hot right now, and one of the most desirable is the Fox bodied Mustang because itÆs cheap and offers incredible bang for the buck. Today on GEARZ, Stacey rolls in a 1990 Mustang cop car and shows how easy it is to pull out an automatic tranny, and stuff in a Tremec 6spd so you can shift the gears properly. Then itÆs off to the Hot Rod Institute in South Dakota to see the paint and body work that the instructors and students have been doing to the V8 Interceptor. If you thought this Æ67 Cougar was wild before, wait ætil you see this show! Here Kitty,ģ..KittyģģKittyģ.
Video.title: Foxbody Mustang Stick Shift Swap & V8 Interceptor Paint and Body

Image.alt: Foxbody Mustang Stick Shift Swap & V8 Interceptor Paint and Body
Foxbody Mustang Stick Shift Swap & V8 Interceptor Paint and Body
Foxbody Mustang Stick Shift Swap & V8 Interceptor Paint and Body

Video.summary: One of the hottest vehicles to hit the gear head world is the Side-by-Side, or Utility Vehicle. Once relegated to just farm or ranch work, the UTV is now something that you will see being used for Hunting, Fishing, Camping, Landscapingģ.virtually ANY kind of outdoor work or recreation. Of course that means the aftermarket is full of things to make an UTV better. Today on GEARZ, Stacey takes a Polaris Ranger and decks it out with a 3 Įö lift, bigger tires and axles, winches front and rear, and all kinds of other cool things to build an all-terrain vehicle that will really shine on the trail or off. After that he gives you a sneak peek at the new Ironworker that has everybody talking. If you work a lot with metal, you need this machine!
Video.title: Modifying a Polaris Ranger UTV/Side-By-Side

Video.summary: Stacey puts a programmer on the Drag æNÆ Wagon to finally bring the stripped down Ram Hot Rod truck to life. If youÆve been wondering what kind of performance potential these new Ram Express trucks have, wait until you see one lit up with a supercharger! Talk about breathing fire! After that Stacey lays out how to set up a shop with custom flooring, custom cabnets, and of course a lot of tools and toolboxes. If youÆve ever wondered about the best way to lay out a shop, and utilize your space, this is the show to seeģ and GEARZ is the show to watch!
Video.title: Drving the Drag N Wagon

Video.summary: Project Heavy Metal is back as Stacey rolls the International Cabover truck into the shop so he can outfit it with a vintage Holmes dual wrecker bed. The big truck continues to come together using parts from a junkyard wrecker, swap meet vintage beer kegs, and some new aftermarket parts. If you always thought that you had to spend big money to build a unique vehicle, this ōHeavy Metalö truck will change your mind, and probably have you digging through junkyards in hopes of finding a vintage truck of your own!
Video.title: Hot Rod Tow Truck

Video.summary: Everybody wants a classic short bed pickup. Unfortunately, short beds are hard to find for a good priceģ but long beds are a dime-a-dozen, and cheap! The solution?... chop a long bed down! That is exactly what Stacey does on this episode of GEARZ. He takes a classic 67 Chevy long bed, and walks you through the way to cut the frame properly, so you have a safe and reliable truck when you are done. If youÆve heard about this kind of modification, but always thought it was too difficult or involved for you to do, you need to watch this episode of GEARZ and let the cutting begin!
Video.title: Long Bed Into Short Bed

Video.summary: Who put men on the moon? If you answered ōGearheadsö then you are rightģ of course, we call them engineers and rocket scientists now. But that doesnÆt change the fact that a bunch of hot rodders figured out how to get America into Space, and Stacey takes a look at the roots of the Space Program, and the invention of the moon buggy. Then itÆs out to the NASA testing grounds to see the annual Moon buggy race. If you think youÆve seen some weird projects, wait until you see what some of these young minds have dreamed up. You may think youÆre on another planet, but youÆre notģ youÆre watching GEARZ!
Video.title: Monn Buggy Race

Video.summary: Stacey picks up right where he left off a few weeks ago and proceeds to install a brand new short bed, on the chassis of a truck that was born a LONG BED. ThatÆs right, with the cutting done, the next step is to assemble a new short bed using all new aftermarket parts. You hear people say, ōif it was that easy, everybody would be doing it!ö Well, it IS that easy, and a lot of people ARE doing itģ and YOU need to be one of them! Nowģ chop to it!
Video.title: Short Bed Classic Truck

Video.summary: It was one of the most lethal airplanes of WWIIģ the P-38 Lightning. Stacey takes a look at what made it so unique and why the German army nicknamed it the ōForked tailed devilö. But the legend didnÆt end in the 1940Æsģ it continues on as Stacey showcases the new P-38 engine parts from Jon Kaase that you can put on your small block Ford to make it run like greased lightning! After that, Stacey sits down with Rock and Roll legend and certified car nut, James Burton, to dig into the unique connection between cars, guitars, and Rock and roll. Turn it onģ crank it upģ and hold on!
Video.title: P38 Lightning

Video.summary: Some of the most neglected tools around the house are lawn and garden tools. Mowers, weed-eaters, chain saws, etc. all usually get thrown in a garage in the fall, never to be touched again until spring. Stacey walks through the simple steps on how to care for those tools, so they give you years of trouble free service. After that, He goes through the many parts you will need to put a manual transmission into a project, with Transmission Tech. Finally, if youÆre looking for a simple project to do over the weekend, how about a muffler swap? You say ōnoö?... Stacey says ōyes.ö
Video.title: Lawn & Garden

Video.summary: The V8 Interceptor is BACK and wearing a striking coat of glossy black paint! How did it happen? Well, Stacey walks you through the process and shows off the incredible paint and body work that was done by the Hot Rod Institute. From the subtle body nips and tucks, to the major fabrication of a new roof, this classic Cougar now has a straighter body than anything that may have rolled off the assembly line in 1967! But the paint is only half the story, as Stacey starts to re-assemble the car with bumpers, lights and engine, you start to get a good look at what is rapidly becoming the ultimate street predator!
Video.title: V8 Interceptor Paint and Body

Video.summary: What is cooler than a vintage tow truck? ģ Nothing! Stacey proves that point by rolling Heavy Metal back into the GEARZ shop to outfit it with new electric winches, and an amazing custom tool box and light bar. This Æ69 International cabover truck may have been rescued from a junkyard, but it wonÆt be long before this truck starts pulling off rescues of its own! This is recycling at itÆs best! Then ride along with Stacey and crew as they travel to Minnesota and get the story on Edwards Manufacturing and how they have managed to stay in business for over a hundred years building simple tools. ItÆs all good, and itÆs all GEARZ!!
Video.title: Heavy Metal Winches

Video.summary: When you say ōHEMIö, you get peopleÆs attention! But when you say ōRay Barton HEMIö you win races. Stacey and crew take you to Ray Barton Racing Engines to show you the amazing engines and parts they build, and why they go so fast. After that, Stacey digs into the interior of the V8 Interceptor and shows what kind of options you have when you are restoring or customizing the interior of your project. YouÆll love the custom PRS guitar and matching wood dash and steering wheel! There is no doubt that this Cougar is the perfect blend of brutal power, and sophisticated elegance.
Video.title: Ray Barton Story

Video.summary: Stacey rolls in SGT. ROCK to take care of steering, brakes, and the exhaust system. With a quick trip to Stainless Works for a custom set of headers and exhaust system, and with the layout of the four-wheel hydraulic steering, it wonÆt be long before this big military beast is rolling under its own power. After that, he takes on the seat in Heavy Metal tow truck. Just because a project may have come out of a junkyard, doesnÆt mean it has to look like junk. Stacey proves that by adding a custom bomber-style seat to this already VERY unique vehicleģ this episode of GEARZ is the one to see!
Video.title: Sgt. Rock Exhaust

Video.summary: Stacey sets out to build a nice hunting rig out of a 2012 GMC 4x4 truck. But this rig belongs to John Godwin of Duck Commander, which means the truck will be spending some serious time on the roadģ and offģ hunting for ducks. On goes a 6ö lift, 35ö tall tires, and massive bumpers and winch to pull this big white beast out of any mud holes it may end up in. And since it IS such a big white beast, it is only fitting that Stacey name this project ōMoby Duckö! If youÆve ever wondered if itÆs possible to have a daily driver, that can also be a nice hunting, fishing, or outdoor rig. This episode of GEARZ will show you how to do it.
Video.title: Moby Duck

Video.summary: Stacey rolls the SL-C back into the shop to show off the twin turbo system Schwartz Performance installed. If you thought 1000hp out of an LS-7 was impossible, THINK AGAIN! Even wilder is the fact that this engine is pulling that kind of horsepower on pump gas! If supercars and twin turbos are your thing, you have to see this! After that, Stacey goes through some of the problem areas that you will face when you are building a vehicle and how to pick the right project. Finally He digs into the 1st motorized vehicle that most of us hadģthe Go-Kart. There's something for everybody in this show. ThatÆs why they call it GEARZ!
Video.title: Super Car, Hot Rods, Go Carts

Image.alt: Super Car, Hot Rods, Go Carts
Super Car, Hot Rods, Go Carts
Super Car, Hot Rods, Go Carts

Video.summary: We dig into the ultimate how-to projectģ building a shop! ItÆs an incredible transformation as Stacey and Crew take a huge open warehouse and build it into the new GEARZ shop. Along the way youÆll pick up all kinds of tips and tricks that Stacey uses when heÆs setting up a useable work space. With todayÆs economy, there are more and more people thinking about opening a shop, or converting their existing garage space into a more useable area. This show can help you do that. After that, Stacey shows how to stop rust dead in its tracks by using the simplest tool in the boxģ a paint brush! ItÆs all here on GEARZ!
Video.title: Building A Shop

Video.summary: Stacey makes a house callģ to Pennsylvania! Why?... to pick up an old fire truck to use as a project truck to make even more house calls with! If this sounds confusing, then you NEED to see this show, because Stacey shows you how to take a vehicle that nobody really has a use for anymore, and convert it into something you can use for your home or business. Sound deadening, tool boxes, and an on-board air system are all things that youÆll see go on as the big green fire truck, begins the conversion into a rolling tool truck
Video.title: The Gearz Express

Video.summary: ItÆs anniversary timeģthe 100th episode of GEARZ! And we have put together a one-hour special of the history and making of GEARZ, in two episodes! Stacey takes a look back at how the show came about. Then he digs into some of the project vehicles, and looks at the ideas and reasoning that shaped these trend-setting vehicles. If youÆre new to GEARZ or wondered how the show was put together, this episode will shed some light on that
Video.title: 100th Episode Pt. 1

Video.summary: Part two of the 100th Episode special starts with a bang and a lot of tire smoke, as Stacey looks back at some of the wild test drives and feature vehicles that have been on GEARZ over the years. After that, they go behind the scenes and show you some of the funny and potentially dangerous situations that can happen when you work on a show like GEARZ. Finally, we showcase some of the incredible guests that have been on the show, to give you an idea of what to expect in the future. ItÆs all here and only on GEARZ!
Video.title: 100th Episode Pt. 2

Video.summary: Stacey rolls in the big green fire truck, with every intention of changing the color and look. But heÆs not using paintģ heÆs using a vinyl wrap! If youÆve ever wondered what the deal is with vinyl wraps and if it is a good thing to do to your project, you need to see this show! But thatÆs not all, as the wrap is going on, Stacey also takes the time to show how to restore and bring back to life your accessories and trim without spending a lot of money.
Video.title: Wrapping The Beast

Video.summary: If youÆve been around cars very much, youÆve heard the term ōbarn findö. It depicts that ōdiamond in the roughö vehicle that everybody wants to find. Stacey delves into the barn find phenomenon and unveils what could be the ultimate barn findģ the lost Richard Petty Car! Follow the incredible story of the car that Richard and Maurice Petty built in their early days, that was then sold and disappeared for decades... only to be found by Stacey and returned to Richard Petty so it could take its rightful place in the Petty Museum. ItÆs gearhead archeology at its best, and youÆll only see it on GEARZ!
Video.title: Richard Petty

Video.summary: Stacey hits the road to visit a GEARZNation member to see if he can lend a hand on his project. The owner is a 16-year-old kid and the subject is his Æ96 Dodge pickup thatÆs looking a little rough from years of use and abuse. That quickly changes as Stacey jumps in to help give the truck a new face, and at the same time offers tips and tricks to breathe a breath of fresh air into the project. See what a difference some time, expertise, and a few parts can make on a beat up old truck! After that, Stacey takes a look at the amazing Moduline Cabinets, and digs into what makes them so great.
Video.title: On The Road

Video.summary: Stacey shows you how to tackle the one restoration project that everybody dreams of doing one day... the frame off restoration! YouÆll be surprised how simple a job like this can be if you follow his simple tips and tricks and plan the project out properly. In the world of high dollar builds, a simple frame off restoration can get you back on the road enjoying your car or truck and at a fraction of the price. But to get a grasp on how to do it, you have to see this show. After that, Stacey swaps some licks with recording artist Casey James who just happens to be a gearhead at heartģ all hereģ all Gearz!
Video.title: LMC Casey James Bronco

Video.summary: So, you think you want some custom bumpers for your big olÆ truck? Stacey shows you how to find what youÆre looking for in the aftermarket, and also how to fabricate and build your own custom mounts to make æem sit exactly where you want them. After that he digs into the history of the Sgt. Rock truck and how it is permanently connected to ōthe greatest generationö. But this truck isnÆt just about WWII, as a special tailgate airbrushed by Mickey Harris reveals, ItÆs about the men and women that have sacrificed for the freedoms we enjoy today. This is one show that will have you waving the flag!
Video.title: Custom Bumper Mounts

Video.summary: Stacey hits the road to help out a Gearz Nation member with his æ56 Corvette project. Since the guy really wants to drive the car, putting in a modern 5 or 6spd tranny would be really nice. But which one? Well, we not only answer that question, but also give the guy a little helping hand with the transmission too. If youÆve ever wished Stacey made house calls, this is the show to see. After that we take a look at some serious questions that people ask about becoming a tool dealer and if itÆs really worth it. You wanted to knowģ we got the answers for you!
Video.title: On The Road Again

Video.summary: Stacey dives into the world of the sleeper, by taking a non-descript Æ73 Plymouth Satellite, and begins transforming it into a street pounding monster under the skin. Gone is the lethargic 318 engine and front suspension, and in its place Stacey begins fitting a new state of the art performance suspension. It you are into surprising people at stop lights and run-what-you-brung race nights, then a ōStreet Sweeperö just might be the sort of approach you want to take on your next project.
Video.title: The Street Sweeper

Video.summary: The Street Sweeper is back! Stacey delves into just how many parts it takes to completely change the drivetrain and suspension on a classic muscle car. Accessories, transmission, overdrive, headers, the list may seem to go on forever, but itÆs all about finding the right parts in the aftermarket, to build the car that YOU want. After that itÆs off to the races to see if E3Æs new top fuel spark plug can handle the pressure of competition. If you want to see the ins and outs of building a car, GEARZ is the show to see!
Video.title: Street Sweeper E3 Testing

Video.summary: If you could only do one upgrade on your vehicle, that would make the biggest impact on the looks and drivability, what would it be? Stacey answers that question by installing a Katzkin leather interior into a Jeep Compass, and showing that if youÆve got an open weekend and a few hundred bucks, you can do the same thing on your car or truck. After that, he jumps back onto the Street Sweeper project to lay out the interior and the rearend, and finally drop that huge Hemi into place. If youÆve ever thought it would be fun to build a sleeper car, this is one Æ73 Plymouth Satellite that shows how it can be done!
Video.title: Street Sweeper Interior

Video.summary: Stacey Preps the bed of the NAPA Perfect additions truck. From rolling out Herculiner to installing transfer tank, truck boxes, and even a combination generator/compressor, this big Ford begins its Perfect Additions transformation. He also throws in a wiring tech that will keep you from getting your wires crossed.
Video.title: NAPA Perfect Additions Truck

Video.summary: Stacey tackles power gains at the air intake, modifies the computer and replaces the stock exhaust before addressing a 2ö front-end suspension lift kit. He moves on to give the truck some extra height and support in the rear of the truck where payload weight can be a serious issue. After the suspension is in place, new wheels and tires bring the truck back down to the ground. Stacey also covers several tools that will help you get that old exhaust out from under your vehicle.
Video.title: NAPA Perfect Additions Truck 2

Video.summary: Stacey leads the crew on a hunt for a junkyard Jaguar with a rare engine. Pin-striping legend Von Hot Rod comes to the GearZ studio and lays down some lines on a hot rod guitar. Need to repair or replace the door on your pick up? StaceyÆs got a handle on that. A bed lifter system turns a four-man operation into a one-man show. And StaceyÆs got a steering column that will keep Falcon owners between the lines. All this and more!
Video.title: Junkyard Jag

Video.summary: Stacey tells the story of the legendary Cheetah and how that car was designed to beat ShelbyÆs famed Cobra. The GEARZ hunt for this iconic race car features the original Æ63 Cheetah that Budd Clusserath drove 215 mph at Daytona in æ64. But the story doesnÆt end there, because Stacey gets his hands on a Cheetah Evolution and shows you how to build your own version of this tire shredding cat! After that, youÆll see Steering wheel options from Grant, a serpentine belt system from LMC Truck, and Wizard products to make your vehicle come alive, as well as a quick tip to get things rolling in your shop. All this and more on GearZ.
Video.title: Evolution Worlds Fastest Cat

Video.summary: Moving deeper into the build of the Cheetah Evolution, Stacey and the crew pull the skin off this cat and start putting in the guts. Overviews of suspension, steering, and brakes get this project up and running. Moving into the heart of this beast, Stacey shows us that good things do come in small packagesŚthe LSX 454 small block is sure to get your pulse rate up. And once the transmission is mounted, look out! Headers follow, and this cat is almost ready to run. StaceyÆs tool tech will give you a liftģ all this and more on GearZ.
Video.title: Put A Heart In The Cat

Image.alt: Put A Heart In The Cat
Put A Heart In The Cat
Put A Heart In The Cat

Video.summary: A road trip, an overview of the Cheetah interior including seat and dash installation, rear lights and a tool tech. The GearZ crew heads out on the prowl to visit a budding young mechanic with a passion for 4X4Æs. Back in the garage, Stacey gets in tight with the Cheetah, installing seats and the dash. Selecting gauges and placement are discussed, an AC unit will help keep this cat cool, taillights are fitted, and the side pipes will give this Cheetah some growl! StaceyÆs tool tech will keep your project on time and on budget. And Fast Orange cleans more than your hands. All this on GearZ.
Video.title: Cheetah Paws

Video.summary: Auction cars and what you might really find out once the car is in your garage: suspension, brakes and electrical concerns. In the current fad of ōflippingö cars for cash, Stacey takes on the good, bad, and ugly of a Æ66 Biscayne that was bought at auction. Nice looking paint canÆt hide the safety issues that are found once this car is put on the lift. From suspension, to brakes, to battery cablesģ and even down to the lug nuts, this auction beauty turns out to be more of a beast. Safety and sport-style handling are only a few repairs away this week on GearZ.
Video.title: Beaten & Downtrodden

Video.summary: Stacey and the crew head north to celebrate 40 years of Heartthrob Exhaust and see just how far this company has come since its modest beginnings in a garage. After that we dig into a cracked dash and show how easy it is to replace one yourself with some aftermarket parts from LMC Truck, and then we look at a painless solution will help clean up years of spliced wires and old electrical tape that might be lurking under your hood. ItÆs simple gearhead stuff that everybody needs to know and itÆs only on GearZ.
Video.title: Automotive Icon Turns 40

Video.summary: Stacey digs back into the Cheetah project by finishing up the stance with custom wheels and tires, and then goes through the ins and outs of putting a classic Hilborn fuel injection on a modern LS engineģ and the dyno numbers show that the engine likes it as much as you will! After that Stacey sits down with a budding car designer that is making a big impact everywhere he goes and thereÆs also a tip on the importance of seals and gaskets. ItÆs all about getting out and turning wrenches, and youÆre only gonna find it on GEARZ!
Video.title: Cheetah Gets Her Growl

Video.summary: Stacey explores the history of the legendary Jaguar D-type racecar and introduces a D-type Jag from Race Car Replicas that is sure to become a legend of its own. From race wheels to classic spoke wheels with true knock-offs, Stacey looks at options for getting the best look and most rubber on the road with this Jag. A tip might keep you from losing your mind when dealing with lug nuts that donÆt want to budge and we talk shop about performance parts for your own cars. So, come hang out while we take on a legendary cat of a different color this week on GearZ!
Video.title: Curves On Wheels
